tools.name: topline_ping
tools.description: Verify the Topline Private Integration Token works and return basic location info. Call this first to confirm setup.

tools.name: topline_setup_check
tools.description: End-to-end setup verification for Topline MCP. Confirms the Private Integration Token is valid, resolves the location, and probes every major scope (contacts, conversations, opportunities, calendars, workflows, forms, surveys, users, custom fields, tags). Returns a structured pass/fail report so you can guide the user to fix any missing scopes. Call this immediately after a client finishes setup.

tools.name: topline_request
tools.description: Generic passthrough to any Topline API v2 endpoint. Use this when no dedicated tool fits. Provide the path (e.g. "/contacts/" or "/opportunities/pipelines") and HTTP method. If the path or query needs a locationId and you don't provide one, the configured sub-account location is used automatically. Returns the parsed JSON response.

path.description: API path, starting with '/'. Example: '/contacts/' or '/opportunities/123'

injectLocationId.description: If true (default), auto-inject locationId into the query string when absent. Set false for endpoints that don't accept it.

tools.name: topline_search_contacts
tools.description: Search contacts by free-text query (name, email, phone) and/or tag. Returns up to 100 per call with a cursor for pagination.

query.description: Free-text search: name, email, or phone

tags.description: Filter to contacts with ALL of these tags

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_get_contact
tools.description: Fetch a single contact by ID. Returns all standard fields and custom fields.

tools.name: topline_create_contact
tools.description: Create a new contact. At minimum provide a name or email or phone. Custom fields go in customFields as {id, value} pairs.

name.description: Full name (use instead of firstName+lastName if you prefer)

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_update_contact
tools.description: Update fields on an existing contact.

tags.description: REPLACES the tag list. Use add/remove tag tools for incremental changes.

tools.name: topline_delete_contact
tools.description: Permanently delete a contact. Irreversible.

tools.name: topline_add_contact_tags
tools.description: Add one or more tags to a contact.

tools.name: topline_remove_contact_tags
tools.description: Remove one or more tags from a contact.

tools.name: topline_upsert_contact
tools.description: Create a contact, or update the existing one if email/phone matches. Use this when you're not sure whether the contact already exists.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_add_contact_to_workflow
tools.description: Enroll a contact in a workflow.

tools.name: topline_remove_contact_from_workflow
tools.description: Remove a contact from a workflow.

tools.name: topline_search_conversations
tools.description: Search conversations for the sub-account. Filter by contact, status, or query.

contactId.description: Only return conversations with this contact

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_get_conversation
tools.description: Fetch a single conversation thread including recent messages.

tools.name: topline_get_messages
tools.description: List messages in a conversation.

tools.name: topline_send_message
tools.description: Send a message (SMS, Email, WhatsApp, or Facebook/Instagram DM) to a contact. The contact must already exist.

html.description: HTML body (Email only, takes precedence over message)

fromNumber.description: Sender phone number (SMS only, must be a number on the sub-account)

tools.name: topline_create_conversation
tools.description: Create a new conversation with a contact (rarely needed — send_message usually creates one implicitly).

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_list_pipelines
tools.description: List all opportunity pipelines in the sub-account with their stages.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_search_opportunities
tools.description: Search opportunities. Filter by pipeline, stage, status, contact, or free text.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_get_opportunity
tools.description: Fetch a single opportunity by ID.

tools.name: topline_create_opportunity
tools.description: Create a new opportunity in a pipeline stage, associated with a contact.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_update_opportunity
tools.description: Update an opportunity — move stages, change value, mark won/lost, etc.

tools.name: topline_delete_opportunity
tools.description: Delete an opportunity.

tools.name: topline_list_calendars
tools.description: List all calendars configured on the sub-account.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_get_calendar_slots
tools.description: Get available time slots for a calendar within a date range (ms epoch).

tools.name: topline_create_appointment
tools.description: Book an appointment on a calendar for a contact.

endTime.description: ISO 8601 end time (optional — inferred from calendar if omitted)

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_update_appointment
tools.description: Update an appointment (reschedule, change status, reassign).

tools.name: topline_delete_appointment
tools.description: Cancel and delete an appointment.

tools.name: topline_get_calendar
tools.description: Get the full calendar definition (availability rules, team members, slot duration, etc.).

tools.name: topline_update_calendar
tools.description: Update a calendar's name, description, slot duration, availability, team members, or event title. Pass only the fields you want to change. The calendar must already exist — calendar CREATE is not available under PIT (marketplace OAuth only).

slug.description: URL slug (shows in public booking URLs)

eventColor.description: Hex color shown in the UI

tools.name: topline_delete_calendar
tools.description: Delete a calendar. All future appointments on this calendar are cancelled. Past appointments are retained as historical records.

tools.name: topline_list_contact_tasks
tools.description: List all tasks for a contact.

tools.name: topline_create_task
tools.description: Create a task associated with a contact.

tools.name: topline_update_task
tools.description: Update a task — change title, body, due date, completion status.

tools.name: topline_delete_task
tools.description: Delete a task.

tools.name: topline_list_contact_notes
tools.description: List all notes on a contact.

tools.name: topline_create_note
tools.description: Create a note on a contact.

userId.description: User ID to attribute the note to

tools.name: topline_update_note
tools.description: Update the body of a note.

tools.name: topline_delete_note
tools.description: Delete a note.

tools.name: topline_list_custom_fields
tools.description: List all custom fields configured on the sub-account (for contacts and opportunities). Useful before creating/updating contacts with custom data.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_get_custom_field
tools.description: Get details of a single custom field.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_create_custom_field
tools.description: Create a new custom field on the sub-account. After creation, the returned `id` can be referenced when setting custom-field values on contacts or opportunities. For SINGLE_OPTIONS / MULTIPLE_OPTIONS / RADIO / CHECKBOX fields, supply `options` as an array of {label, value} pairs.

dataType.description: Field data type. One of: TEXT | LARGE_TEXT | NUMERICAL | PHONE | MONETORY | CHECKBOX | SINGLE_OPTIONS | MULTIPLE_OPTIONS | DATE | TEXTBOX_LIST | RADIO | FILE_UPLOAD | SIGNATURE.

model.description: Object this field applies to: 'contact' or 'opportunity'. Default 'contact'.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown on the field UI

options.description: For SINGLE_OPTIONS / MULTIPLE_OPTIONS / RADIO / CHECKBOX: list of pickable values.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_update_custom_field
tools.description: Update a custom field's name / options / placeholder. Pass only the fields you want to change.

options.description: Full replacement list for option-typed fields.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_delete_custom_field
tools.description: Delete a custom field. This removes the field definition but does not affect contact/opportunity rows that already have a value stored — those become orphaned data accessible only via raw_payload.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_list_custom_values
tools.description: List all sub-account custom values. Use before setting workflow merge tags or composing messages that reference {{custom_values.X}}.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_get_custom_value
tools.description: Get details of one custom value by id.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_create_custom_value
tools.description: Create a new sub-account custom value. Returns the created record including its id and the auto-generated merge-tag key ({{custom_values.<slug>}}).

value.description: The value to store. Used verbatim when merge tags are expanded.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_update_custom_value
tools.description: Update an existing custom value's name and/or value. Pass only the fields you want to change.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_delete_custom_value
tools.description: Delete a sub-account custom value. Any merge tags referencing the deleted key will render empty in workflows/messages.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_list_workflows
tools.description: List all workflows in the sub-account. Returns workflow IDs, names, and status.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_list_tags
tools.description: List all tags available on the sub-account.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_create_tag
tools.description: Create a new tag on the sub-account. Tags are referenced by NAME from contact.tags JSON arrays, so use a concise human-readable name.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_update_tag
tools.description: Rename a tag. Existing contacts tagged with the old name get the rename transitively.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_delete_tag
tools.description: Delete a tag from the sub-account. Contacts that were tagged lose the tag reference; contact records themselves are not touched.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_get_location
tools.description: Fetch full details of the current sub-account (location) — name, address, timezone, business info.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_list_users
tools.description: List users on the sub-account. Useful for finding user IDs to assign tasks, opportunities, or appointments.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_get_user
tools.description: Fetch a single user by ID.

tools.name: topline_list_forms
tools.description: List all forms on the sub-account.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_list_form_submissions
tools.description: List submissions for a specific form.

startAt.description: ISO date — only submissions after this date

endAt.description: ISO date — only submissions before this date

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_list_surveys
tools.description: List all surveys on the sub-account.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_list_survey_submissions
tools.description: List submissions for a specific survey.

locationId.description: Location (sub-account) ID. Defaults to TOPLINE_LOCATION_ID env var if omitted.

tools.name: topline_describe_data_catalog
tools.description: COMPLETE catalog of every GHL/Topline object that exists upstream — including objects we sync (queryable via SQL), objects we've catalogued but haven't built sync for yet, objects that require OAuth/agency scopes our PIT auth can't reach, and objects we've declined to sync. Call this when topline_describe_schema doesn't show something the user is asking about — it will tell you whether the data lives on disk (queryable now), is pending (answer will lag a sync cycle), or is inaccessible (tell the user to request it instead of inventing a workaround). Contrast with topline_describe_schema, which is SCHEMA-only and hides everything not currently exposed. Returns { entries: [{ name, category, status, description, sql_table?, endpoint?, notes? }...] } with status in {exposed, syncing, catalogued, requires_oauth, inaccessible, declined}.

tools.name: topline_describe_schema
tools.description: Overview of every table currently exposed to SQL queries in this sub-account's data warehouse. Returns table names, one-line descriptions, row counts, and a short SQLite-dialect cheat sheet. Call this FIRST when the user asks anything analytics-flavored ('how many', 'group by', 'trend', 'compare', 'duplicate'). It's cheap and tells you what's queryable. Follow up with topline_explain_tables on the ones you want to use, then topline_execute_query with the SQL.

tools.name: topline_explain_tables
tools.description: Per-column detail for one or more tables returned by topline_describe_schema. Gives column names, SQLite types, nullability, JSON-column flags, enum values for closed-set text columns, foreign-key hints for joins, and approximate row counts. Call this before writing a SELECT so your WHERE / JOIN clauses use real column names and valid enum values. Rejects tables that aren't in the exposed set.

tables.description: One or more table names to explain

tools.name: topline_execute_query
tools.description: Run a read-only SQL query against this sub-account's SQLite warehouse. ONE SELECT or WITH...SELECT statement at a time. DDL, DML, PRAGMA, ATTACH, and admin commands are rejected by the parser before they reach the database. Results are capped at 5000 rows; larger result sets come back with truncated: true. Returns { columns, rows, elapsed_ms, truncated, effective_limit, rewritten_sql }. SQLite dialect — no DATE_TRUNC (use strftime), JSON columns need json_extract / json_each, timestamps are ISO 8601 strings that compare lexicographically.

query.description: A single SELECT or WITH ... SELECT statement. Example: "SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS n FROM opportunities GROUP BY status ORDER BY n DESC"

tools.name: topline_utilize_api
tools.description: Describes the HTTP query API you can point Looker Studio, Retool, Lovable, Claude Code, n8n, or curl at for live-data dashboards. Returns URL shapes, auth format, example curl commands, and guidance for wiring up a dashboard. Use this when the user asks about building dashboards, embedding the data in another tool, or when a query is too complex for chat and they'd rather save it as a saved view.

tools.name: topline_find_references
tools.description: Answer 'what uses X?' across synced CRM objects. Closed-enum dispatcher — runs hard-coded SQL per kind; no arbitrary SQL accepted. Supported kinds: tag, custom_field, custom_value, pipeline, pipeline_stage, calendar, user, contact, opportunity, form, survey. WORKFLOWS are NOT supported: GHL's public API does not expose workflow internals, so we cannot know which workflows reference any given object. kind=opportunity returns ONLY direct references (the opportunity itself, its pipeline, stage, and contact) — it does NOT include downstream activity like messages or calls, since those belong to the contact and may span multiple opportunities. To get a contact's full activity, call this tool again with kind=contact using the contact_id from the opportunity's extra payload. Results are capped at 500 rows total (not per kind); truncated=true means upstream had more. For tags, accepts either the tag id or the tag name.

id.description: ID of the object to search references for (or name, for tags)
